search_queries_used: site:bsg.org.uk chronic diarrhoea adults history examination investigations guideline
site:dha.gov.ae gastroenteritis adults diarrhoea referral telehealth 2024
candidate_sources_rejected: acute infectious guidance treated as complete chronic diarrhoea evidence
automatic infection, IBS, IBD, malabsorption, medicine, stool test, imaging, endoscopy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Acute and chronic diarrhoea have different scopes.
No cause or action is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: No legacy item in gp-diarrhea-follow-up-in-gp remains source-supported after conservative correction; item-specific population applicability was not independently established without new research.
setting_applicability: No legacy item in gp-diarrhea-follow-up-in-gp remains source-supported after conservative correction; transfer from the recorded source setting to the exact workflow item was not independently established.
UAE_applicability: insufficient_evidence for every removed gp-diarrhea-follow-up-in-gp legacy-item mapping; UAE applicability requires independent item-level review before any mapping is reinstated.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact chronic-diarrhoea section supports interval duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and clinician-performed examination without assigning cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and physical examination.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording already reviewed blood, stool, FIT, coeliac, endoscopy, imaging, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption context without ordering tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of existing blood, stool, FIT, endoscopy, imaging, coeliac, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA referral section supports clinician-entered safety-net, referral status, questions, and follow-up for qualified adult acute contexts without assigning urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current stool frequency and form, hydration, bleeding, travel, examination, cause, diagnosis, test, treatment, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
